leads.profitability_check: PROFITABLE — FY2024 revenue RMB 777.1bn (+23% YoY), overseas revenue RMB 221.9bn; net profit ~RMB 40bn (approx., widely reported)

evidence: BYD India registered 2007 in Chennai with an electronics manufacturing plant; India listed among BYD's 30+ industrial-park countries alongside US, Brazil, Hungary

First BYD K9 pure-electric bus arrived at Port of Chennai Aug 2013; BYD-Olectra partnership holds ~52% share of commercially operating e-buses in India; buses run in 10+ cities (Mumbai, Hyderabad, Pune, Kerala)
Cumulative India investment >$150m across two factories as of 2023
PN3 ceiling: the proposed $1bn BYD-Megha Engineering EV/battery JV (Telangana, 2023) was REJECTED by the Indian government after security-related deliberations under Press Note 3
2024 overseas passenger-NEV sales 417k units (+71.9% YoY) — APAC expansion is an explicit strategy (Thailand plant operating, Indonesia under construction)
india_apac_interest.india_operations: BYD India Pvt Ltd (Chennai) — electronics + EV assembly (Atto 3/Seal CKD), e-bus chassis via Olectra tie-up; expansion capped by PN3 (greenfield car-plant proposal rejected; press reports of a revived Hyderabad-area plant plan remain unapproved)
india_apac_interest.report_used: BYD 2024 Annual Results (HKEX filing, en.byd.com/bydukmedia summary) + BYD India corporate disclosures; full HKEX AR PDF not parsed page-by-page

leads.profitability_check: MARGINALLY PROFITABLE — FY2024 revenue RMB 627.59bn (-15.7% YoY), net profit RMB 1.666bn (-88.2% YoY) per SAIC Annual Report 2024 (saicmotor.com English PDF)

evidence: MG Motor India (Halol, Gujarat plant) restructured 2023 into JSW MG Motor India JV — JSW took 35% at ~$1.2bn valuation, the flagship PN3-era 'Indianization' of a Chinese OEM
2026 reports: SAIC selling a further 10% to JSW (JSW 35%→45%, SAIC 49%→39%), making JSW the largest shareholder; SAIC to reinvest ~Rs 6bn ($63m) of proceeds into new-model launches
Coverage explicitly ties the dilution to 'tighter scrutiny of Chinese investments in India' — Indian-majority ownership unlocks future investment flexibility
SAIC keeps supplying platforms/tech (Windsor EV etc.) — a technology-partner rather than owner posture
india_apac_interest.india_operations: JSW MG Motor India (SAIC minority partner post-dilution), Halol manufacturing plant, growing EV lineup; SAIC transitioning from controlling owner to technology/minority partner under PN3 pressure
india_apac_interest.report_used: SAIC Motor Annual Report 2024 (English, saicmotor.com) for financials; stake-sale details from Autocar India/Yahoo Finance (May-Jun 2026), not yet in an annual report

leads.sector: EV Batteries (listed under White Goods & Electricals in candidate CSV)
leads.profitability_check: PROFITABLE — FY2024 revenue ~RMB 362bn, net profit ~RMB 50.7bn (approx., widely reported); world's largest EV-battery maker

evidence: Reliance-CATL talks on a technology-transfer arrangement for domestic Indian cell production 'did not result in an agreement'; Reliance pivoted to sourcing pre-made cells for BESS assembly

CATL's LRS (Licensing, Royalty, Service) model — 'patent licenses, technology, and support to car manufacturers and battery companies worldwide' — is exactly the PN3-compatible structure, discussed with about a dozen OEMs, but no India LRS deal has closed
Beijing's tightening export controls on core battery-manufacturing technology now block the India licensing route from the CHINESE side as well
APAC/overseas build-out directed at Indonesia, Hungary, Spain (Stellantis JV) — not India
india_apac_interest.india_operations: No Indian entity or plant identified; interest is latent (licensing-model fit) but blocked bilaterally — PN3 on India's side, tech-export controls on China's side
india_apac_interest.report_used: No India mention traced to CATL's annual report; evidence from CnEVPost, discoveryalert.com.au (Reliance talks), CATL official LRS statements — secondary sources, UNVERIFIED against the AR text

leads.profitability_check: PROFITABLE — FY2024 revenue ~$37.3bn (up from $32.7bn in 2023), '2024 Annual Results: Sustained Revenue and Net Profit Growth' (net profit ~RMB 13.4bn approx.)

evidence: AR-level footprint statement: manufacturing bases in 'China, India, and Vietnam' (~420,000 employees globally)
Luxshare India Co., Ltd. established Feb 2019 (pre-PN3), acquiring a defunct Tamil Nadu digital-products plant with planned Rs 7.5bn (~RMB 649m) investment to make Apple iPhone components
Wingtech ODM acquisition disclosure explicitly includes 'the relevant business asset packages of India Wingtech Co., Ltd.' — an indirect, acquisition-route deepening of India exposure
2025-26 press repeatedly links Luxshare to Apple's India supply-chain shift (incl. reported JV discussions with Indian EMS players such as Motherson — NOT confirmed in any filing found)
india_apac_interest.india_operations: Luxshare India Pvt Ltd (Tamil Nadu) operating as an Apple-chain component site (grandfathered pre-PN3); India Wingtech assets being folded in via the Wingtech ODM deal
india_apac_interest.report_used: Luxshare Annual Report 2024 (Chinese PDF via 10jqka, not parsed in full) + Wingtech acquisition disclosure excerpts + Tracxn entity record for Luxshare India Pvt Ltd

leads.company: Hangzhou Hikvision Digital Technology Co., Ltd.
leads.profitability_check: PROFITABLE — FY2024 overseas revenue RMB 25.99bn (+8.39% YoY, 28.1% of total) per 2024 Annual Report (English PDF, hikvision.com); net profit ~RMB 12bn (approx.)

evidence: Prama Hikvision India Pvt Ltd (est. 2009, Mumbai) — Indian-majority JV, the archetypal PN3-compatible structure: manufacturing done under an Indian partner's majority
Prama Hikvision FY2024: operating revenue >INR 500 crore, revenue +3.84%, profit +115%; ~1,569 employees; electronic components & equipment manufacturing in India
2024 AR: overseas main + innovative business revenue combined surpassed domestic main business for the first time — structural pivot to overseas markets incl. South Asia
Counterweight: Hikvision faces Indian government procurement restrictions/security scrutiny (trusted-source rules for surveillance gear), capping addressable market
india_apac_interest.india_operations: Prama Hikvision India Pvt Ltd — local manufacturing (Vasai plant) + nationwide distribution; Hikvision is minority technology partner in an Indian-controlled JV
india_apac_interest.report_used: Hikvision 2024 Annual Report (English PDF, hikvision.com) + Prama Hikvision MCA-derived financials (thecompanycheck/Tofler)

leads.profitability_check: PROFITABLE — FY2024 revenue ~RMB 286bn, net profit ~RMB 18.7bn (approx.); AR 2024 confirms revenue and net-profit growth

evidence: AR 2024 (English, smart-home.haier.com): South Asia revenue RMB 11.53bn, +21.05% YoY; 'Indian market... revenue growing by 30%... revenue grew over 30% to USD 1 billion for the first time' in Haier's 20th anniversary year in India
India is Haier Group's third-largest market after China and the US
Dec 2025: Bharti Enterprises + Warburg Pincus agreed to buy 49% of Haier India for ~$2bn (Haier retains 49%, management 2%); Beijing cleared the sale Dec 2025
Coverage explicitly frames the deal as PN3 relief: 'diversified governance and indirect localization' to escape case-by-case Chinese-FDI approval
india_apac_interest.india_operations: Haier Appliances India — Pune and Greater Noida manufacturing parks, $1bn revenue, moving to a 49/49/2 Sino-Indian ownership structure to neutralize PN3 constraints
india_apac_interest.report_used: Haier Smart Home Annual Report 2024 (English H-share PDF, smart-home.haier.com/media/adrjuuk3/h24ar-report.pdf); stake sale from Bloomberg/Business Standard (Dec 2025), will appear in AR 2025/2026

leads.profitability_check: PROFITABLE — FY2024 revenue ~RMB 409bn, net profit ~RMB 38.5bn (approx., dual-listed SZSE/HKEX, consistently profitable)

evidence: Committed >Rs 13.5bn (~$190m) to the Midea Science & Technology Park near Pune (~270,000 sqm; capacity 500k each refrigerators/washers/purifiers/heaters, 1.5m residential ACs, 250k commercial ACs, 4.5m compressors; 2,000+ jobs) — announced pre-PN3, operational since ~2020
Carrier Midea India JV (Bangalore) — ACs under an existing foreign-JV wrapper, another PN3-tolerant structure
Midea India Pvt Ltd FY2025: revenue +74.4%, profit +201.4% (company-registry data)
Press reports (2025-26) of Midea exploring an Indian-partner stake structure similar to Haier's — UNVERIFIED in filings
india_apac_interest.india_operations: Midea India Pvt Ltd (Haryana, est. 2017) + Pune Science & Technology Park + Carrier Midea India JV; grandfathered pre-2020 investments now the growth platform since fresh Chinese capital needs approval
india_apac_interest.report_used: Midea Group AR not directly parsed; evidence from Pandaily investment announcement, Carrier Midea India investor page, Midea India registry financials (thecompanycheck)

leads.company: Gree Electric Appliances, Inc. of Zhuhai
leads.profitability_check: PROFITABLE — FY2024 revenue RMB 190.04bn (-7.3% YoY), net profit RMB 32.185bn (+10.9% YoY) per 2024 Annual Report (English version, gree.com.cn)

evidence: English AR extracts name Southeast Asia, Middle East, Europe, North America as growth markets and new plants in Brazil and Pakistan — India conspicuously absent from the manufacturing map
India presence is dealer/distributor-only (IndiaMART/Justdial networks across Delhi, Mumbai, Ahmedabad etc.) — an export model requiring no PN3 approval
Pre-2020 Gree India manufacturing intentions never materialized post-PN3; Pakistan (also a Haier/Gree base) serves as its South Asia manufacturing foothold instead
india_apac_interest.india_operations: No Indian manufacturing entity identified; brand present via import-distribution only
india_apac_interest.report_used: Gree 2024 Annual Report (English version, gree.com.cn PDF); India dealer footprint from trade directories

leads.profitability_check: PROFITABLE — FY2024 revenue ~RMB 182bn; FY2023 net profit RMB 16.82bn (+3.6%); FY2024 net profit ~RMB 13bn (approx.); world's largest MDI/TDI supplier (4.91m t isocyanate capacity end-2024)

evidence: Company profile/AR materials list 'over ten branches across Europe, the Middle East, America, Japan, Russia, and India' — India is an explicitly named overseas branch geography (Wanhua Chemical India Pvt Ltd, Mumbai sales office)
India is a major growth import market for MDI/TDI (polyurethanes for appliances/construction/footwear) where Wanhua is a leading supplier via exports
No Indian manufacturing announced; overseas plants are Hungary (BorsodChem) and planned US/Middle East — sales-office-plus-exports is the PN3-frictionless model for chemicals
india_apac_interest.india_operations: Wanhua Chemical India Pvt Ltd (Mumbai) sales/marketing branch; supplies MDI/TDI/polyols to Indian converters; no production assets
india_apac_interest.report_used: Wanhua annual report/company publications (whchem.com) branch-network statement; financials from 2023 AR disclosure + 2024 results coverage (ECHEMI)

leads.profitability_check: PROFITABLE — FY2024 net profit ~RMB 7.4bn (approx.); FY2023 exports +46.6% to 5.84m t, record; Q1 2025 profit -8.6% on weak demand

evidence: Stated strategy: 'strategically expanding its markets in South and Southeast Asia' with exports targeted at 10m t/yr by 2028 — South Asia is a named export destination, i.e., India interest is as a customer market, not an investment destination

First greenfield overseas project is a $1bn plate mill in SAUDI ARABIA; company says the model will be replicated in 'One Belt One Road countries across Southeast Asia and North Africa' — India not on the list
India's steel safeguard duties on Chinese imports (2025) and PN3 make both the trade and investment channels hostile
india_apac_interest.india_operations: No Indian entity or plant; exports of high-value flat products into South Asia only, and those face Indian safeguard/anti-dumping barriers
india_apac_interest.report_used: Baosteel results coverage and company statements via SteelOrbis/SteelRadar/gmk.center; annual report itself not parsed — treat India-specific absence as UNVERIFIED-by-AR

leads.profitability_check: PROFITABLE — 2024 annual results strong (net profit ~RMB 49bn approx., freight-rate driven); 'global digital supply chain operation and investment platform' positioning per 2024 results announcement

evidence: COSCO/OOCL liner networks call Indian ports (Nhava Sheva, Mundra, Chennai) as routine commercial operations; OOCL India and COSCO Shipping Lines India agency entities long predate PN3 — presence is service-based, needing no FDI approval

2024 AR port-investment actions went to Egypt (Ain Sokhna), Thailand (Laem Chabang), Piraeus, Abu Dhabi — no Indian terminal investment (Chinese port investment in India is effectively impossible under PN3 plus port-sector security rules)
Group affiliate COSCO Shipping International (Singapore) states ambition to be 'the best-integrated logistics service provider in South and Southeast Asia'
india_apac_interest.india_operations: Agency/liner-service presence only (COSCO Shipping Lines India, OOCL India — entity details UNVERIFIED this session); zero terminal/asset investment in India, by structural necessity
india_apac_interest.report_used: COSCO SHIPPING Holdings 2024 Annual Results announcement (en.hold.coscoshipping.com); COSCO Shipping International (Singapore) AR 2024; India-specific text not found in AR extracts

leads.company: Shenzhen Mindray Bio-Medical Electronics Co., Ltd.
leads.profitability_check: PROFITABLE — FY2024 revenue RMB 36.73bn (+5.1% YoY); net profit ~RMB 11.7bn (approx.); FY2023 revenue RMB 34.93bn (+15%)

evidence: Earnings-call/AR materials: 'developing countries experienced growth exceeding 30%' — the company's fastest-growing segment, with India its largest developing-market healthcare opportunity

39 international subsidiaries in 30+ countries incl. Asia; Mindray maintains an India arm (mindray.com/in, Mindray Medical India Pvt Ltd — entity UNVERIFIED this session) selling patient monitoring, IVD, imaging
Model is sales-subsidiary + imports, not manufacturing — low PN3 friction; but Indian public-procurement 'Make in India'/local-content rules increasingly disadvantage imported Chinese devices
india_apac_interest.india_operations: Sales/service subsidiary; no Indian manufacturing found; growth via distribution into hospitals and labs
india_apac_interest.report_used: Mindray FY2023 & 1Q2024 earnings-call script (mindray.com IR PDF) + annual report summaries; India-specific revenue not broken out

geopolitical_note: Press Note 3 (April 2020) requires prior Government of India approval for any FDI from land-border countries, and approvals for Chinese proposals are rare and slow. This reshapes what 'India investment interest' can look like for Chinese corporates: (1) outright greenfield/JV proposals get rejected — BYD-Megha's $1bn EV plant (2023) is the canonical case; (2) incumbents 'Indianize' — SAIC diluted MG Motor India to JSW (49%→39% and falling), Haier sold 49% of Haier India to Bharti/Warburg for ~$2bn (Dec 2025, cleared by Beijing) explicitly to escape PN3 friction; (3) Indian-majority JV fronts carry the manufacturing — Prama Hikvision; (4) technology licensing/royalty (CATL's LRS model) is the theoretically PN3-compatible route but is now ALSO constrained from the Chinese side by Beijing's export controls on battery/rare-earth/process technology (the Reliance-CATL talks died partly on this); (5) pre-2020 grandfathered assets (Midea's Pune park, Luxshare's Tamil Nadu plant, BYD Chennai) become the only expansion platforms; (6) asset-light models — export/dealer networks (Gree, Baosteel, Wanhua sales office) and services (COSCO liner calls) — proceed freely because they involve no Indian FDI. Net effect: annual reports of Chinese majors show India as a fast-growing REVENUE geography (Haier +30%, 'developing countries' +30% for Mindray) while capex conspicuously routes to Vietnam, Thailand, Indonesia, Brazil, Hungary, Saudi Arabia instead.
method_notes: All 12 companies verified present in candidates_cn.csv (3,514 names) before inclusion; tickers quoted as listed in the CSV (note BYD and COSCO SHIPPING Holdings appear via their SEHK lines 1211/1919; both are dual A+H listed)
Sector labels follow the CSV's FocusSector column; CATL sits under 'White Goods & Electricals' in the CSV though its real sector is EV batteries — flagged in-line

Evidence gathered via ~13 web searches surfacing English annual reports (Haier H-share AR 2024, Hikvision 2024 AR, SAIC AR 2024, Gree 2024 AR English) plus reputable secondary coverage quoting AR figures; cninfo.com.cn Chinese PDFs were NOT parsed page-by-page — where a claim could not be traced to a report it is marked UNVERIFIED
Profitability figures: revenue/segment numbers marked 'per AR' are sourced; net-profit figures marked 'approx.' are FY2024 consensus-reported values not re-verified against filings this session
SAIC is included despite an 88% FY2024 profit collapse because it remained net-profitable and is the single most instructive PN3 case (MG→JSW MG)

Goertek was screened but excluded from the final 12 (present in CSV, profitable, but capex is overwhelmingly Vietnam-directed with no substantive India evidence) to keep sector spread at 8 of the 12 focus sectors within the 10-12 lead budget

Selection covers: Auto/EV (BYD, SAIC), Batteries (CATL), Electronics (Luxshare, Hikvision), White Goods (Haier, Midea, Gree), Chemicals (Wanhua), Steel (Baosteel), Marine (COSCO), Medical Devices (Mindray)
site_access_issues: cninfo.com.cn not fetched directly (Chinese-language PDF repository, anti-bot; all A-share AR evidence came via mirrored English ARs, IR-site PDFs, or press quoting the reports)
Large AR PDFs (200-600 pages: BYD HKEX filing, Luxshare 10jqka PDF, COSCO AR) were located but not parsed in full — India keyword sweeps of full Chinese texts (印度) not performed
COSCO Shipping Lines India / OOCL India and Mindray India legal entities asserted from general knowledge, not verified against registry sources this session — marked UNVERIFIED in-line
Reported Luxshare-Motherson and Midea-Indian-partner JV talks found only in press rumor form; no filing evidence — excluded from evidence weighting
